content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Apr 5, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day saw the opening of two new pull requests for plugin additions. Significant discussions continued on active issues concerning agent identity, trust layers, and capability token enforcement, with a focus on aligning behavioral fingerprinting and concrete implementation sketches.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
No completed work to report for this period.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
- A new plugin, `@madeonsol/plugin-madeonsol`, has been proposed for addition to the registry [#334](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/334).
- Another new plugin, `plugin-signalfuse`, has been submitted for inclusion [#333](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/333).

## CLOSED ISSUES
No issues were closed during this period.

## NEW ISSUES
No new issues were opened during this period.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
### Agent Identity and Trust Layer Discussions
Discussions on the "Plugin Proposal: AgentID — Cryptographic Identity & Trust Layer for ElizaOS Agents" [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6688) continued with detailed comparisons to existing systems. @haroldmalikfrimpong-ops provided corrections regarding AgentID's dynamic trust levels and behavioral baseline monitoring, emphasizing that trust profiles are not static and adapt in real-time. They also highlighted AgentID's use of JCS RFC 8785 canonicalized, Ed25519 dual-signed receipts with policy hash chaining. Previously, @pshkv outlined the similarities and differences with SINT Protocol's Avatar Layer, particularly their CSML (Composite Safety-Model Latency) for behavioral fingerprinting, and expressed interest in aligning behavioral fingerprinting approaches. @majorelalexis-stack also contributed by mentioning MAXIA's implementation of W3C DIDs and on-chain escrow for trust levels.

### Capability Token Enforcement for Tool Calls
The "Plugin proposal: @sint/eliza-plugin — capability token enforcement for Eliza agent tool calls" [#6707](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6707) saw a concrete implementation sketch from @pshkv. The sketch details how the plugin would hook into Eliza's `IAgentRuntime` using `processActions` middleware, enforcing capability tokens via `PolicyGateway.enforce()` before tool execution. It also provided an example of how to configure the plugin in `eliza.config.ts` and outlined what parts of the SINT Protocol are already built. Questions were raised for maintainers regarding the appropriate hook, plugin location (monorepo vs. standalone), and the preferred method for submitting a full implementation.

### Cross-Agent Messaging and Identity
The previously closed issue "[Feature] Cross-Agent Messaging (Agent-to-Agent)" [#6418](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6418) was revisited by @HavenCTO, who closed it as "not planned" in favor of focusing on wallet-native identity as the foundational element for agent-to-agent coordination. They emphasized that XMTP is crucial for cryptographic identity, not just messaging, and indicated that they would rather contribute to foundational identity work than maintain a separate messaging solution. They also mentioned building a similar system at github.com/Haven-hvn/haven-core.
